skill.name: nano-banana
skill.description: Generate images with Google Gemini native image models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Gemini 3 Pro Image, Gemini 2.5 Flash Image. Capabilities: text-to-image, image editing, multi-image input.
skill.summary: Native image generation using Google Gemini models through the inference.sh CLI with support for text-to-image and image editing.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: This skill is documentation (SKILL.md) for using the inference.sh CLI to generate images with Google Gemini models. Static analysis detected 30 external command patterns and 9 URLs, but these are all Markdown documentation examples, not executable code. The skill uses allowed-tools restriction to only execute infsh commands. External API calls to inference.sh are required for core functionality. Low risk due to external command execution and network access, but patterns are legitimate and restricted.

low_findings.title: Pipe to shell installation pattern
low_findings.description: Installation command uses curl piped to shell (curl | sh) which downloads and executes a script from the network. This is a standard pattern but requires trust in the source domain.

content.user_title: Generate Images with Google Gemini Models
content.value_statement: Create professional images from text descriptions using Google Gemini native image models. Access high-quality image generation through a simple CLI interface with support for editing and customization.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using Gemini 3 Pro Image and Gemini 2.5 Flash Image models
Edit existing images by providing input images and modification prompts
Generate multiple image variations in a single request
Customize output aspect ratio and resolution
Enable Google Search grounding for real-time information in generated images
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and authentication
Depends on external Google Gemini API availability and rate limits
Image generation is not instantaneous - processing time varies by model and resolution
Cannot generate images locally - all processing happens on Google servers

use_cases.title: Marketing Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate custom visuals for social media posts, blog headers, and advertisements without stock photo dependencies.

use_cases.title: Design Prototyping
use_cases.description: Quickly visualize design concepts, logos, and illustrations during the ideation phase before committing to detailed work.

use_cases.title: Creative Exploration
use_cases.description: Experiment with different artistic styles, compositions, and visual concepts for personal or professional projects.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a tropical beach at sunset with palm trees and calm ocean waves, photorealistic style
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate a simple image from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Multiple Variations
prompt_templates.prompt: Create 4 different logo concepts for a tech startup named 'Nova' - minimalist style, blue and white color scheme
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate multiple options to choose from

prompt_templates.title: Image Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a golden retriever puppy sitting on the grass in the foreground of this image, maintain the same lighting and style
prompt_templates.scenario: Modify an existing image with new elements

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Customization
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a cyberpunk cityscape at night in 16:9 aspect ratio, 4K resolution, with neon signs, flying vehicles, and dramatic atmospheric lighting, digital art style
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a complex image with specific technical requirements

output_examples.input: A futuristic cityscape at sunset with flying cars
output_examples.output: High-resolution image showing a sprawling city with tall buildings, warm sunset colors in the sky, and vehicles flying between structures

output_examples.input: Minimalist logo design for a coffee shop (4 variations)
output_examples.output: Four distinct logo designs featuring coffee-related imagery in clean, minimalist style with consistent branding elements

output_examples.input: Edit this landscape photo: add a rainbow in the sky
output_examples.output: The original landscape image with a naturally-placed rainbow arcing across the sky, matching the lighting conditions

best_practices: Use specific, descriptive prompts that include style, lighting, composition, and key details for better results
Start with Gemini 2.5 Flash Image for faster iterations, then use Gemini 3 Pro Image for final high-quality outputs
When editing images, clearly describe what to change while specifying what elements to preserve from the original
anti_patterns: Avoid overly complex prompts with too many conflicting elements - focus on one clear subject and style
Do not expect exact text rendering in generated images - AI image models struggle with precise typography
Avoid using copyrighted character names or trademarked elements in prompts for commercial projects

faq.question: What is the difference between Gemini 3 Pro Image and Gemini 2.5 Flash Image?
faq.answer: Gemini 3 Pro Image produces higher quality results with better detail but takes longer to generate. Gemini 2.5 Flash Image is faster and still produces excellent results, making it ideal for iterations and quick prototyping.

faq.question: Do I need to install any software to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the inference.sh CLI tool and authenticate with your account. Run the install command from the Quick Start section to get started.

faq.question: Can I edit my own images with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you can provide image URLs in the images parameter along with a prompt describing the changes you want to make. You can include up to 14 input images.

faq.question: What image formats are supported for output?
faq.answer: The skill supports common output formats. Use the output_format parameter to specify your preferred format. Refer to the inference.sh documentation for available format options.

faq.question: How do I enable Google Search grounding?
faq.answer: Set enable_google_search to true in your request. This allows the model to access real-time information from Google Search when generating images, useful for current events or factual accuracy.

faq.question: What are the costs associated with using this skill?
faq.answer: Image generation uses Google Gemini models through inference.sh platform. Pricing depends on the model used and image resolution. Check the inference.sh pricing page for current rates and your account limits.
